description:
The diagram illustrates the dependency model of CV Assistant, showing how Skills are unlocked by Powers and Models. The layout is primarily top-to-bottom, with three main sections: SKILLS, POWERS, and MODELS, stacked vertically. A soft, light-grey background is used for the entire figure.

**SKILLS (Top Section):**
This section is enclosed in a rounded-corner rectangle with a very light sky-blue fill (opacity ~10-15%) and a thin, solid dark-blue border. The title "SKILLS (20 composable capabilities)" is prominently displayed at the top, in bold sans-serif font, slightly larger than the skill names. Inside this section, the skills are grouped into four sub-categories: "Vision Skills", "Content Skills", "Research Skills", and "ML / Training Skills". Each sub-category has a bold sans-serif title. Each skill within a sub-category is represented by a rounded rectangle with a white fill and a thin, light-grey border. Vision Skills include "SAM3 Segment Anything", "OCR · Text Extraction", "Object Detection", "Object Tracking", "Text → Image Generation", "2D Image Processing", "3D Image Processing", "Video Understanding", "Image Stitching", "Super Resolution", and "Image Denoising". Content Skills include "Write Research Blog", "Weekly Digest", and "Email Reports". Research Skills include "Paper → Spec", "Knowledge Graph", and "Equation Extraction". ML / Training Skills include "Model Fine-Tuning", "Dataset Analysis", and "Kaggle Competition". Skills that require specific Powers or Models have annotations indicating these dependencies (e.g., "requires SAM3 model weights", "requires Email SMTP power") in a smaller sans-serif font. Small, relevant icons are placed to the left of some skills: a camera icon for Vision Skills, a document icon for Content Skills, a magnifying glass for Research Skills, and a chip icon for ML/Training Skills.

**POWERS (Middle Section):**
This section is enclosed in a rounded-corner rectangle with a very light peach fill (opacity ~10-15%) and a thin, solid dark-orange border. The title "POWERS (external integrations that unlock skills)" is prominently displayed at the top, in bold sans-serif font, slightly larger than the power names. Each power is represented by a rounded rectangle with a white fill and a thin, light-grey border. Example powers include "HuggingFace Hub token", "Google Gemini API key", "Email SMTP credentials", "Kaggle API key", "Brave Search API key", "Semantic Scholar API key", and "GitHub token". Each power has a small key icon to its left.

**MODELS (Bottom Section):**
This section is enclosed in a rounded-corner rectangle with a very light mint-green fill (opacity ~10-15%) and a thin, solid dark-green border. The title "MODELS (AI weights providing intelligence)" is prominently displayed at the top, in bold sans-serif font, slightly larger than the model names. Each model is represented by a rounded rectangle with a white fill and a thin, light-grey border. Example models include "SAM3 (facebook/sam3, 6.9 GB)", "SAM 2.1 Large (facebook/sam2.1-hiera-large, 2.5 GB)", "SD-Turbo (stabilityai/sd-turbo, 4.8 GB)", "SDXL-Turbo (stabilityai/sdxl-turbo, 6.5 GB)", "DeepGen 1.0 (deepgenteam/DeepGen-1.0, 16.4 GB)", "PaddleOCR (pip package, 0.5 GB, auto-downloads)", and "Ollama models (qwen2.5-coder, qwen3-vl)". Each model entry includes the model name, source, and size, all in a standard sans-serif font. A small brain icon is placed to the left of each model.

**CONNECTIONS:**
Dashed arrows with a medium line thickness connect the SKILLS section to the POWERS and MODELS sections. Arrows originate from skills that require specific powers or models and point to the corresponding power or model. For example, "SAM3 Segment Anything" has a dashed arrow pointing to "SAM3 (facebook/sam3, 6.9 GB)" in the MODELS section, "OCR · Text Extraction" has a dashed arrow pointing to "PaddleOCR (pip package, 0.5 GB, auto-downloads)" in the MODELS section, "Text → Diagram" has a dashed arrow pointing to "Google Gemini API key" in the POWERS section, "Model Fine-Tuning" has a dashed arrow pointing to "HuggingFace Hub token" in the POWERS section, "Train CV Model" has a dashed arrow pointing to "Kaggle API key" in the POWERS section, and "Extract Data from GitHub" has a dashed arrow pointing to "GitHub token" in the POWERS section. The arrows are dark-grey.

**Additional Annotations:**
- "Object Detection" requires torchvision (BSD-3) + RT-DETR from transformers.
- "Object Tracking" requires supervision + ByteTrack/SORT (MIT).
- "Text → Image Generation" requires diffusers + SD-Turbo/SDXL-Turbo model weights.
- "Video Understanding" is unlocked by Vid-LLM power.
- "Write Research Blog" uses ArXiv + web_search tools.
- "Paper → Spec" is ready (fetch_arxiv_paper + extract_equations).
- "Knowledge Graph" is ready (Obsidian vault).
- "Equation Extraction" is ready.
- "Model Fine-Tuning" is ready with HF_TOKEN power; streams training via SSE.
- "Dataset Analysis" is ready.
- "Email Reports" requires Email SMTP power configured.
- "Kaggle Competition" requires Kaggle API power.
- "Ollama models (qwen2.5-coder, qwen3-vl)" enables all LLM/VLM reasoning.

**Overall Styling:**
The diagram has a clean and professional look, adhering to the "Soft Tech & Scientific Pastels" aesthetic. The color palette uses soft pastel shades (sky-blue, peach, mint-green) for section backgrounds and slightly darker shades (dark-blue, dark-orange, dark-green) for borders. The fonts are clear and readable (sans-serif). Icons are simple and consistent. All boxes have rounded corners. The layout is clean and well-balanced.

critic_suggestions: Add a dashed arrow from "Text → Diagram" to "Google Gemini API key" in the POWERS section.
Add a dashed arrow from "Model Fine-Tuning" to "HuggingFace Hub token" in the POWERS section.
Add a dashed arrow from "Kaggle Competition" to "Kaggle API key" in the POWERS section.
Add a dashed arrow from "Extract Data from GitHub" to "GitHub token" in the POWERS section.
Add a dashed arrow from "SAM3 Segment Anything" to "SAM3 (facebook/sam3, 6.9 GB)" in the MODELS section.
Add a dashed arrow from "OCR · Text Extraction" to "PaddleOCR (pip package, 0.5 GB, auto-downloads)" in the MODELS section.
